target.title: Feat/sandbox docker volumes strategies
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds optional `sandbox.docker.volumes` support for Docker sandboxes with `ephemeral`, `named`, and `bind` strategies.
- Hardens Docker volume validation so bind-volume sources use structured host-path validation instead of legacy colon parsing, and rejects Docker `--mount` field injection through comma-delimited source/target values.
- Makes writable anonymous and named Docker volumes usable at brand-new target paths under the default non-root sandbox user by initializing ownership before the sandbox process drops privileges.
- Keeps existing `sandbox.docker.binds` behavior compatible; this is additive for config users.

## Files touched
- `src/config/types.sandbox.ts`, `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`, `src/config/validation.ts`, `src/agents/sandbox/config.ts`: config contract, schema validation, and global/per-agent merge behavior for `sandbox.docker.volumes`.
- `src/agents/sandbox/docker.ts`, `src/agents/sandbox/docker-backend.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.shared.ts`: Docker `--mount` wiring, volume ownership initialization, explicit non-root `docker exec` user handling, and config-hash recreation for writable Docker volumes.
- `src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.ts`: runtime security checks for bind-volume source paths, reserved targets, blocked host paths, and mount-field injection.
- `extensions/openshell/src/backend.ts`, `src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.ts`: explicit rejection on backends that do not mount Docker volumes.
- `docs/gateway/sandboxing.md`: user documentation for the new volume config and security behavior.

## Compatibility and security
- Backward compatible: yes. Existing `sandbox.docker.binds` configs remain valid and unchanged.
- New secrets or token handling: no.
- New network behavior: no.
- New runtime permission shape: Docker containers with writable anonymous/named volumes start with root plus `CHOWN`, `SETUID`, and `SETGID` only long enough to create/chown configured writable volume targets, then `exec setpriv` drops to the sandbox user with `CapEff: 0000000000000000`.
- Existing containers with writable anonymous/named Docker volumes are recreated through a conditional config-hash epoch so they do not keep pre-fix root-owned target directories.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `sandbox.docker.volumes` now supports ephemeral, named, and bind strategies; writable anonymous/named Docker volumes mounted at brand-new target paths are writable by the non-root sandbox user after container startup.

Real environment tested: Docker Desktop 4.73.0 / Docker Engine 29.4.3 with `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a Docker sandbox container with `--read-only`, `--cap-drop ALL`, writable anonymous volume target `/proof/ephemeral`, writable named volume target `/proof/named`, root-only ownership initialization, and `setpriv` drop to `1000:1001`; then ran `docker exec --user 1000:1001` to write result files into both new volume targets.

Evidence after fix:
```text
uid=1000(sandbox) gid=1001(sandbox) groups=1001(sandbox)
CapEff: 0000000000000000
drwxr-xr-x 2 sandbox sandbox ... /proof/ephemeral
drwxr-xr-x 2 sandbox sandbox ... /proof/named
ephemeral=ephemeral-ok
named=named-ok
```

Observed result after fix: both new volume target paths were owned by `sandbox:sandbox`, the non-root sandbox user wrote successfully to both mounted volumes, and the final exec had no effective capabilities.

What was not tested: full production multi-node rollout and broad full CI; validation used focused unit/type checks plus local real Docker behavior proof.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.build-docker-exec-args.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/docker-backend.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts src/config/config.sandbox-docker.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/browser.create.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/backend.test.ts`
  - `13 passed`, `205 passed`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.build-docker-exec-args.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/docker-backend.test.ts`
  - `5 passed`, `45 passed`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --pretty false`
  - exit `0`
- `git diff HEAD --check`
  - clean
target.author: sasan1200
